q: In circular motion, there are two different forces that are encountered.
Centripetal force is the force acting towards the centre of the circular path around which the object is moving.
The centrifugal force appears to act in a direction pointing towards the centre.
The person pulls the string to exert an inward force on the ball.
There is a misconception that this reaction force is the outward-directed centrifugal force acting on the ball.
Except centripetal force, there are many other forces acting on the ball along the string.
When the car is moving with constant velocity along a straight road, there is no net force on the person.
When the car moves around a curve the person feels an outward force exerted on him.
The centrifugal force is a real force.
It is an apparent or fictitious force which is equal and opposite to the centripetal force.
A centrifuge is a piece of equipment that puts an object in rotation around a fixed axis.
In a laboratory centrifuge that uses sample test tubes, the radial acceleration causes denser particles to rise to the top of the tube.
Centrifugal pump is a hydraulic machine which converts mechanical energy into hydraulic energy.
The centrifugal action of a washing machine is used in spin drying the clothes.
The centrifugal governor is a device used to measure and regulate the speed of a machine, such as an engine.
A satellite orbiting a planet is supported by the centrifugal force.
